Allows you to assign `scopeNames`/retokenize the captured text  
Only applies to `"end"`  
Overrides `"captures"`

Controls if the `"end"` pattern should attempt to match before or after the `"patterns"` array  
`0`, `false` and `null` will disable it. `true` and numbers != `0` will enable it
Same as `"name"`, but only applies to inside a `"begin"` - `"end"`/`"while"` region

suggestions.label: patterns
suggestions.documentation: An array of pattern's to include

suggestions.label: captures
suggestions.documentation: Allows you to assign scopeNames/retokenize the captured text

documentation.value:
A single scopeName to be assigned to the provided token.  
Overrides `"name"`.  
Does not support separating scopes by spaces.  
_Not currently supported by VSCode_

suggestions.documentation: Alternative comment; because VSCode ignores duplicate `//` keys. Supports all json types

documentation.value:
Disables the current rule for easy testing  
_Not currently supported by VSCode_

suggestions.label: include
suggestions.documentation: Reference an item in a repository

suggestions.label: repository
suggestions.documentation:
A dictionary of rules
https://code.visualstudio.com/api/language-extensions/syntax-highlight-guide#contributing-a-basic-grammar

documentation.value:
Allows you to assign `scopeNames`/retokenize the captured text  
Only applies to `"while"`  
Overrides `"captures"`

suggestions.documentation: Target specific capture group
Alternative comment; because VSCode ignores duplicate `//` keys. Supports all json types

suggestions.label: name
suggestions.documentation:
A list of space-separated scope-names to be assigned to the provided token. VSCode will then colour that token using the current theme
`comment`, `string` and `regex` disables bracket matching while `meta.embedded` reenables it
https://macromates.com/manual/en/language_grammars#naming_conventions

suggestions.label: patterns
suggestions.documentation: An array of pattern's to include

suggestions.label: repository
suggestions.documentation:
A dictionary of rules
https://code.visualstudio.com/api/language-extensions/syntax-highlight-guide#contributing-a-basic-grammar
Alternative comment; because VSCode ignores duplicate `//` keys. Supports all json types

documentation.value:
Disables the current rule for easy testing  
_Not currently supported by VSCode_

suggestions.label: "comment"
suggestions.documentation: For comments

suggestions.label: "comment.block"
suggestions.documentation: Multi-line comments like /* … */ and <!-- … -->

suggestions.label: "comment.block.documentation"
suggestions.documentation: Embedded documentation

suggestions.label: "comment.line"
suggestions.documentation: Line comments, we specialize further so that the type of comment start character(s) can be extracted from the scope

suggestions.label: "comment.line.character"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of line comments

suggestions.label: "comment.line.double-dash"
suggestions.documentation: -- comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.double-slash"
suggestions.documentation: // comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.number-sign"
suggestions.documentation: # comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.percentage"
suggestions.documentation: % comment

suggestions.label: "constant"
suggestions.documentation: Various forms of constants

suggestions.label: "constant.character"
suggestions.documentation: Those which represent characters, e.g. <, \e, \031

suggestions.label: "constant.character.escape"
suggestions.documentation: Escape sequences like \e would be constant.character.escape

suggestions.label: "constant.language"
suggestions.documentation: Constants (generally) provided by the language which are “special” like true, false, nil, YES, NO, etc

suggestions.label: "constant.numeric"
suggestions.documentation: Those which represent numbers, e.g. 42, 1.3f, 0x4AB1U

suggestions.label: "constant.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other constants, e.g. colors in CSS

suggestions.label: "entity"
suggestions.documentation: An entity refers to a larger part of the document, for example a chapter, class, function, or tag. We do not scope the entire entity as entity.* (we use meta.* for that). But we do use entity.* for the “placeholders” in the larger entity, e.g. if the entity is a chapter, we would use entity.name.section for the chapter title

suggestions.label: "entity.name"
suggestions.documentation: We are naming the larger entity

suggestions.label: "entity.name.function"
suggestions.documentation: The name of a function

suggestions.label: "entity.name.section"
suggestions.documentation: The name is the name of a section/heading

suggestions.label: "entity.name.tag"
suggestions.documentation: A tag name

suggestions.label: "entity.name.type"
suggestions.documentation: The name of a type declaration or class

suggestions.label: "entity.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other entities

suggestions.label: "entity.other.attribute-name"
suggestions.documentation: The name of an attribute (mainly in tags)

suggestions.label: "entity.other.inherited-class"
suggestions.documentation: The superclass/baseclass name

suggestions.label: "invalid"
suggestions.documentation: Stuff which is “invalid”

suggestions.label: "invalid.deprecated"
suggestions.documentation: For deprecated stuff e.g. using an API function which is deprecated or using styling with strict HTML

suggestions.label: "invalid.illegal"
suggestions.documentation: Illegal, e.g. an ampersand or lower-than character in HTML (which is not part of an entity/tag)

suggestions.label: "keyword"
suggestions.documentation: Keywords (when these do not fall into the other groups)

suggestions.label: "keyword.control"
suggestions.documentation: Mainly related to flow control like continue, while, return, etc

suggestions.label: "keyword.operator"
suggestions.documentation: Operators can either be textual (e.g. or) or be characters

suggestions.label: "keyword.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other keywords

suggestions.label: "markup"
suggestions.documentation: This is for markup languages and generally applies to larger subsets of the text

suggestions.label: "markup.bold"
suggestions.documentation: Bold text (text which is strong and similar should preferably be derived from this name)

suggestions.label: "markup.heading"
suggestions.documentation:
A section header. Optionally provide the heading level as the next element, for example markup.heading.2.html for

…

in HTML

suggestions.label: "markup.italic"
suggestions.documentation: Italic text (text which is emphasized and similar should preferably be derived from this name)

suggestions.label: "markup.list"
suggestions.documentation: List items

suggestions.label: "markup.list.numbered"
suggestions.documentation: Numbered list items

suggestions.label: "markup.list.unnumbered"
suggestions.documentation: Unnumbered list items

suggestions.label: "markup.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other markup constructs

suggestions.label: "markup.quote"
suggestions.documentation: Quoted (sometimes block quoted) text

suggestions.label: "markup.raw"
suggestions.documentation: Text which is verbatim, e.g. code listings. Normally spell checking is disabled for markup.raw

suggestions.label: "markup.underline"
suggestions.documentation: Underlined text

suggestions.label: "markup.underline.link"
suggestions.documentation: This is for links, as a convenience this is derived from markup.underline so that if there is no theme rule which specifically targets markup.underline.link then it will inherit the underline style

suggestions.label: "meta"
suggestions.documentation: The meta scope is generally used to markup larger parts of the document. For example the entire line which declares a function would be meta.function and the subsets would be storage.type, entity.name.function, variable.parameter etc. and only the latter would be styled. Sometimes the meta part of the scope will be used only to limit the more general element that is styled, most of the time meta scopes are however used in scope selectors for activation of bundle items. For example in Objective-C there is a meta scope for the interface declaration of a class and the implementation, allowing the same tab-triggers to expand differently, depending on context

suggestions.label: "storage"
suggestions.documentation: Things relating to “storage”

suggestions.label: "storage.modifier"
suggestions.documentation: A storage modifier like static, final, abstract, etc

suggestions.label: "storage.type"
suggestions.documentation: The type of something, class, function, int, var, etc

suggestions.label: "string"
suggestions.documentation: Strings

suggestions.label: "string.interpolated"
suggestions.documentation: Strings which are “evaluated”: `date`, $(pwd)

suggestions.label: "string.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of strings (should rarely be used)

suggestions.label: "string.quoted"
suggestions.documentation: Quoted strings

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.double"
suggestions.documentation: Double quoted strings: "foo"

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of quoting: $'shell', %s{...}

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.single"
suggestions.documentation: Single quoted strings: 'foo'

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.triple"
suggestions.documentation: Triple quoted strings: """Python"""

suggestions.label: "string.regexp"
suggestions.documentation: Regular expressions: /(\w+)/

suggestions.label: "string.unquoted"
suggestions.documentation: For things like here-docs and here-strings

suggestions.label: "support"
suggestions.documentation: Things provided by a framework or library should be below support

suggestions.label: "support.class"
suggestions.documentation: When the framework/library provides classes

suggestions.label: "support.constant"
suggestions.documentation: Constants (magic values) provided by the framework/library

suggestions.label: "support.function"
suggestions.documentation: Functions provided by the framework/library. For example NSLog in Objective-C is support.function

suggestions.label: "support.other"
suggestions.documentation: The above should be exhaustive, but for everything else use support.other

suggestions.label: "support.type"
suggestions.documentation: Types provided by the framework/library, this is probably only used for languages derived from C, which has typedef (and struct). Most other languages would introduce new types as classes

suggestions.label: "support.variable"
suggestions.documentation: Variables provided by the framework/library. For example NSApp in AppKit

suggestions.label: "variable"
suggestions.documentation: Variables. Not all languages allow easy identification (and thus markup) of these

suggestions.label: "variable.language"
suggestions.documentation: Reserved language variables like this, super, self, etc

suggestions.label: "variable.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other variables, like $some_variables

suggestions.label: "variable.parameter"
suggestions.documentation: When the variable is declared as the parameter

Replaced with the corresponding capture's captured text.
All leading dots (.) are stripped.

Converts all the alphabetic characters in a capture to UPPERCASE or lowercase.
All leading dots (.) are stripped.

Controls if the `"end"` pattern should attempt to match before or after the `"patterns"` array  
`0`, `false` and `null` will disable it. `true` and numbers != `0` will enable it
Same as `"name"`, but only applies to inside a `"begin"` - `"end"`/`"while"` region

suggestions.label: name
suggestions.documentation:
A list of space-separated scope-names to be assigned to the provided token. VSCode will then colour that token using the current theme
`comment`, `string` and `regex` disables bracket matching while `meta.embedded` reenables it
https://macromates.com/manual/en/language_grammars#naming_conventions

suggestions.label: patterns
suggestions.documentation: An array of pattern's to include

suggestions.label: captures
suggestions.documentation: Allows you to assign scopeNames/retokenize the captured text

documentation.value:
A single scopeName to be assigned to the provided token.  
Overrides `"name"`.  
Does not support separating scopes by spaces.  
_Not currently supported by VSCode_

suggestions.documentation: Alternative comment; because VSCode ignores duplicate `//` keys. Supports all json types

documentation.value:
Disables the current rule for easy testing  
_Not currently supported by VSCode_

suggestions.label: include
suggestions.documentation: Reference an item in a repository

suggestions.label: repository
suggestions.documentation:
A dictionary of rules
https://code.visualstudio.com/api/language-extensions/syntax-highlight-guide#contributing-a-basic-grammar

documentation.value:
Allows you to assign `scopeNames`/retokenize the captured text  
Only applies to `"while"`  
Overrides `"captures"`

suggestions.label: "comment"
suggestions.documentation: For comments

suggestions.label: "comment.block"
suggestions.documentation: Multi-line comments like /* … */ and <!-- … -->

suggestions.label: "comment.block.documentation"
suggestions.documentation: Embedded documentation

suggestions.label: "comment.line"
suggestions.documentation: Line comments, we specialize further so that the type of comment start character(s) can be extracted from the scope

suggestions.label: "comment.line.character"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of line comments

suggestions.label: "comment.line.double-dash"
suggestions.documentation: -- comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.double-slash"
suggestions.documentation: // comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.number-sign"
suggestions.documentation: # comment

suggestions.label: "comment.line.percentage"
suggestions.documentation: % comment

suggestions.label: "constant"
suggestions.documentation: Various forms of constants

suggestions.label: "constant.character"
suggestions.documentation: Those which represent characters, e.g. <, \e, \031

suggestions.label: "constant.character.escape"
suggestions.documentation: Escape sequences like \e would be constant.character.escape

suggestions.label: "constant.language"
suggestions.documentation: Constants (generally) provided by the language which are “special” like true, false, nil, YES, NO, etc

suggestions.label: "constant.numeric"
suggestions.documentation: Those which represent numbers, e.g. 42, 1.3f, 0x4AB1U

suggestions.label: "constant.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other constants, e.g. colors in CSS

suggestions.label: "entity"
suggestions.documentation: An entity refers to a larger part of the document, for example a chapter, class, function, or tag. We do not scope the entire entity as entity.* (we use meta.* for that). But we do use entity.* for the “placeholders” in the larger entity, e.g. if the entity is a chapter, we would use entity.name.section for the chapter title

suggestions.label: "entity.name"
suggestions.documentation: We are naming the larger entity

suggestions.label: "entity.name.function"
suggestions.documentation: The name of a function

suggestions.label: "entity.name.section"
suggestions.documentation: The name is the name of a section/heading

suggestions.label: "entity.name.tag"
suggestions.documentation: A tag name

suggestions.label: "entity.name.type"
suggestions.documentation: The name of a type declaration or class

suggestions.label: "entity.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other entities

suggestions.label: "entity.other.attribute-name"
suggestions.documentation: The name of an attribute (mainly in tags)

suggestions.label: "entity.other.inherited-class"
suggestions.documentation: The superclass/baseclass name

suggestions.label: "invalid"
suggestions.documentation: Stuff which is “invalid”

suggestions.label: "invalid.deprecated"
suggestions.documentation: For deprecated stuff e.g. using an API function which is deprecated or using styling with strict HTML

suggestions.label: "invalid.illegal"
suggestions.documentation: Illegal, e.g. an ampersand or lower-than character in HTML (which is not part of an entity/tag)

suggestions.label: "keyword"
suggestions.documentation: Keywords (when these do not fall into the other groups)

suggestions.label: "keyword.control"
suggestions.documentation: Mainly related to flow control like continue, while, return, etc

suggestions.label: "keyword.operator"
suggestions.documentation: Operators can either be textual (e.g. or) or be characters

suggestions.label: "keyword.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other keywords

suggestions.label: "markup"
suggestions.documentation: This is for markup languages and generally applies to larger subsets of the text

suggestions.label: "markup.bold"
suggestions.documentation: Bold text (text which is strong and similar should preferably be derived from this name)

suggestions.label: "markup.heading"
suggestions.documentation:
A section header. Optionally provide the heading level as the next element, for example markup.heading.2.html for

…

in HTML

suggestions.label: "markup.italic"
suggestions.documentation: Italic text (text which is emphasized and similar should preferably be derived from this name)

suggestions.label: "markup.list"
suggestions.documentation: List items

suggestions.label: "markup.list.numbered"
suggestions.documentation: Numbered list items

suggestions.label: "markup.list.unnumbered"
suggestions.documentation: Unnumbered list items

suggestions.label: "markup.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other markup constructs

suggestions.label: "markup.quote"
suggestions.documentation: Quoted (sometimes block quoted) text

suggestions.label: "markup.raw"
suggestions.documentation: Text which is verbatim, e.g. code listings. Normally spell checking is disabled for markup.raw

suggestions.label: "markup.underline"
suggestions.documentation: Underlined text

suggestions.label: "markup.underline.link"
suggestions.documentation: This is for links, as a convenience this is derived from markup.underline so that if there is no theme rule which specifically targets markup.underline.link then it will inherit the underline style

suggestions.label: "meta"
suggestions.documentation: The meta scope is generally used to markup larger parts of the document. For example the entire line which declares a function would be meta.function and the subsets would be storage.type, entity.name.function, variable.parameter etc. and only the latter would be styled. Sometimes the meta part of the scope will be used only to limit the more general element that is styled, most of the time meta scopes are however used in scope selectors for activation of bundle items. For example in Objective-C there is a meta scope for the interface declaration of a class and the implementation, allowing the same tab-triggers to expand differently, depending on context

suggestions.label: "storage"
suggestions.documentation: Things relating to “storage”

suggestions.label: "storage.modifier"
suggestions.documentation: A storage modifier like static, final, abstract, etc

suggestions.label: "storage.type"
suggestions.documentation: The type of something, class, function, int, var, etc

suggestions.label: "string"
suggestions.documentation: Strings

suggestions.label: "string.interpolated"
suggestions.documentation: Strings which are “evaluated”: `date`, $(pwd)

suggestions.label: "string.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of strings (should rarely be used)

suggestions.label: "string.quoted"
suggestions.documentation: Quoted strings

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.double"
suggestions.documentation: Double quoted strings: "foo"

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other types of quoting: $'shell', %s{...}

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.single"
suggestions.documentation: Single quoted strings: 'foo'

suggestions.label: "string.quoted.triple"
suggestions.documentation: Triple quoted strings: """Python"""

suggestions.label: "string.regexp"
suggestions.documentation: Regular expressions: /(\w+)/

suggestions.label: "string.unquoted"
suggestions.documentation: For things like here-docs and here-strings

suggestions.label: "support"
suggestions.documentation: Things provided by a framework or library should be below support

suggestions.label: "support.class"
suggestions.documentation: When the framework/library provides classes

suggestions.label: "support.constant"
suggestions.documentation: Constants (magic values) provided by the framework/library

suggestions.label: "support.function"
suggestions.documentation: Functions provided by the framework/library. For example NSLog in Objective-C is support.function

suggestions.label: "support.other"
suggestions.documentation: The above should be exhaustive, but for everything else use support.other

suggestions.label: "support.type"
suggestions.documentation: Types provided by the framework/library, this is probably only used for languages derived from C, which has typedef (and struct). Most other languages would introduce new types as classes

suggestions.label: "support.variable"
suggestions.documentation: Variables provided by the framework/library. For example NSApp in AppKit

suggestions.label: "variable"
suggestions.documentation: Variables. Not all languages allow easy identification (and thus markup) of these

suggestions.label: "variable.language"
suggestions.documentation: Reserved language variables like this, super, self, etc

suggestions.label: "variable.other"
suggestions.documentation: Other variables, like $some_variables

suggestions.label: "variable.parameter"
suggestions.documentation: When the variable is declared as the parameter

Replaced with the corresponding capture's captured text.
All leading dots (.) are stripped.

Converts all the alphabetic characters in a capture to UPPERCASE or lowercase.
All leading dots (.) are stripped.
